threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the 'Overground', not the 'Ginger', and wait in line for coffee like it's a normal thing.
Caught a glimpse of a bloke singing opera to a crowd outside Brixton tube at 2am. Never knew night buses came with live performances.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I spotted that stray cat routinely taking the same seat on the bus every morning—like it owned the place—was when I felt at home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can find spontaneous techno parties in abandoned buildings, complete with local food trucks serving vegan curry. It's pure Berlin energy.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know the secret shortcuts around the BART delays; tourists just wait clueless at the station, smartphone in hand.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy get on the 31 with a full-sized piano. He played a tune while we made the slow crawl down Market. That's San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just pop into a bar and find a table at any hour. Most locals plan their drinks around siesta time and busy evening slots.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their choice of paella place; they always pick the one with the tourist menu tucked away in the corner.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it's normal to ask for the Wi-Fi password at a pub. Real locals just accept the signal's as sketchy as the flat whites.
Caught the N35 last night, and a cat was meowing like it was holding a grudge against the bus driver. The weirdest soundtrack to 2 a.m. chaos.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I walked past the Kottbusser Tor and caught a whiff of fresh falafel mixed with that unmistakable scent of spiced curry – that's when I knew.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find a bar that serves drinks in used tin cans—kind of grimy but oddly charming. It's like a pop-up nostalgia trip.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they switch from BART to a bus mid-commute and don’t blink at extra delays. Tourists just look lost.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a taco costume trying to board the 21 bus while juggling burritos. Classic Tenderloin—only in SF can you get dinner and a show.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists flock to Parc Güell thinking it's the essence of Gaudí, but they miss the real vibe in the corner bars where locals swap gossip over vermut.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they’re new by the way they still take photos of the modernista buildings like Gaudí's Casa Batlló, instead of just rushing past.
